title: [BUG] Prometeheus high metric cardinality for certain labels
author: nvtkaszpir
body:
## Have you read through available documentation, open Github issues and Github Q&A Discussions?

Yes

## System information
k8s 1.34.6 so MooseFS runs as statefulset (separate master, metaloggers, chunkservers) and with csi-moosefs all in the same cluster

When using [prometheus-operator](https://github.com/prometheus-operator/prometheus-operator) and thus using ServiceMonitor pointing into a moosefs GUI, then prometheus scrapes the metrics - all fine.

#### Your moosefs version and its origin (moosefs.com, packaged by distro, built from source, ...).
4.59.2 community edition, from images which are available from official Docker Hub https://hub.docker.com/u/moosefs

#### Operating system (distribution) and kernel version.
does not matter

#### Hardware / network configuration, and underlying filesystems on master, chunkservers, and clients.
does not matter

#### How much data is tracked by moosefs master (order of magnitude)?

does not really matter that much, you can test it on 6 nodes with 1 iso split to 10 chunks

 - All fs objects:
 - Total space:
 - Free space:
 - RAM used:
 - last metadata save duration:

## Describe the problem you observed.

I noticed certain metric labels have an always changing values due to the fact that the label values are based on timestamp formatted as string.
This leads to the high metric cardinality for given metric label.

Example:
```
mfs_info_general_misc{strver="4.59.2",version="4.59.2",last_metadata_save_time_str="Mon May 18 17:00:00 2026",last_metadata_save_status_txt="Saved in background"} 1

mfs_info_masters_misc{ip="10.230.82.169",state="MASTER",strver="4.59.2",version="4.59.2",localtime_str="Mon May 18 17:53:11 2026",last_metadata_save_time_str="Mon May 18 17:00:00 2026",last_metadata_save_status_txt="Saved in background",last_metadata_save_checksum="E2386364",exports_checksum="FEE5017509C855FA"} 1

mfs_info_chunk_loop_misc{start_time_str="Mon May 18 17:45:15 2026",end_time_str="Mon May 18 17:50:15 2026"} 1

mfs_info_fs_loop_misc{start_time_str="Mon May 18 16:33:39 2026",end_time_str="Mon May 18 17:28:16 2026"} 1


```


Problematic metric name: `mfs_info_masters_misc`,`mfs_info_general_misc`
label names:
- `exports_checksum`
- `last_metadata_save_checksum`
- `last_metadata_save_time_str`
- `localtime_str`

Problematic metric name: `mfs_info_chunk_loop_misc`,`mfs_info_fs_loop_misc`
label names:
- `end_time_str`
- `start_time_str`


#### Can you reproduce it? If so, describe how. If not, describe troubleshooting steps you took before opening the issue.

This **does not require kubernetes** or prometheus-operator at all, you can use normal prometheus with static config as described in the blog post, or just read gui metrics endpoint directly.

For kubernetes, set up basic moosefs cluster and use servicemonitor as below:

ServiceMonitor spec (nothing special, really):
```yaml
---
apiVersion: monitoring.coreos.com/v1
kind: ServiceMonitor
metadata:
  name: moosefs
  labels:
    app.kubernetes.io/component: metrics
    app.kubernetes.io/instance: moosefs
    app.kubernetes.io/name: moosefs
    release: kube-prometheus-stack
spec:
  endpoints:
    - port: gui
      path: /metrics
      interval: 15s
      scheme: http
  selector:
    matchLabels:
      app.kubernetes.io/component: gui
      app.kubernetes.io/instance: moosefs
      app.kubernetes.io/name: moosefs
```


**Workaround** -  metric relabelings to drop certain labels:

```yaml
---
apiVersion: monitoring.coreos.com/v1
kind: ServiceMonitor
metadata:
  name: moosefs
  labels:
    app.kubernetes.io/component: metrics
    app.kubernetes.io/instance: moosefs
    app.kubernetes.io/name: moosefs
    release: kube-prometheus-stack
spec:
  endpoints:
    - port: gui
      path: /metrics
      interval: 15s
      scheme: http
      metricRelabelings:
        # mfs_info_masters_misc, mfs_info_general_misc
        - action: labeldrop
          regex: (exports_checksum|last_metadata_save_checksum|last_metadata_save_time_str|localtime_str)
        # mfs_info_chunk_loop_misc, mfs_info_fs_loop_misc
        - action: labeldrop
          regex: (end_time_str|start_time_str)
      relabelings:
        - replacement: redacted
          targetLabel: cluster
  selector:
    matchLabels:
      app.kubernetes.io/component: gui
      app.kubernetes.io/instance: moosefs
      app.kubernetes.io/name: moosefs


```

Proper fix:
- expose new metrics and do not use label values with changing values, but just use metric value with a timestamp value - thus just the metric value (and nothing else) will change frequently).

#### Include any warning/errors/backtraces from the system logs.
There are none, because this affects system outside of moosefs.

title: [BUG] background chunk tests doesn't work at all, chunks are never checked
author: StellaZIT
body:
Background test loop on chunkservers doesn't seem to work.

Tested on version 4.58.3 (**lab**) and 4.58.4 (**live**)

On **live** instance (very low usage, about few GB/day of data read) just observing average HDD read values indicate that nothing happens beside normal client usage.
Chunkservers are small, about 300GB of data each, but with default **HDD_TEST_SPEED** set to 1 MB/s and **HDD_MIN_TEST_INTERVAL** set to default 1 day test loop should check ~84 GB of data per day. With 300 GB of chunks there should be constant average minimum 1 MB/s. of disk reads. System tools show only client reads with many hours of inactivity.

On **lab** instance we tested it even further. On 25 test chunkservers with 50GB of space per server, used by chunks about 60-80% available space we randomly damaged dozens of chunks (writing random zeroes to *.mfs files). With same defaults all chunks should be tested in maximum 24 h + time for tests at 1 MB/s, but after 3 days of just waiting (without any client reads) nothing happens, none of chunks were detected as damaged, none of them were read and disk activity was close to zero all the time.
Reading all data from that instance detected all problems in chunks with data, but damaged chunks held as EC code are still there without detection (which is logic, because reading data doesn't read repair data if not needed).

Looking at the code **hdd_tester_thread** is finding all chunks needed to be checked, puts them on check list, but in **hdd_int_chunk_testmove** chunk **testtime** is set to **now**, and that's before **hdd_int_test** is run, where condition **lasttesttime+MinTimeBetweenTests<=now** is never true, because **lasttesttime** is always close to **now**.

Tried to find when it was broken, but looking at the sources up to last 3.0 version looks like logic even then was very similar so I gave up, because I'm missing something obvious, or I don't understand how this background test check should work.

Enabling **HDD_TESTER_DEBUG** didn't help at all - got huge log of new events in looplog.txt (about 1000/sec.). Same results when I tried  to debug it in different places of code - **chunks testtime** are kept close to **now** refreshing every full loop.

Another thing is, that **testtime** is only held in memory (correct me if I'm wrong) and never written to disk. So in large instances, when check loop will need months of full round, restarting server before it ends can make some chunks never checked (depending of luck in random order).

Best regards.

title: [BUG] moosefs-client won't install on Fedora 44: expects libfuse3.so.3 but libfuse3.so.4 is available
author: wrobelda
body:
## Have you read through available documentation, open Github issues and Github Q&A Discussions?

Yes

## System information

#### Your moosefs version and its origin (moosefs.com, packaged by distro, built from source, ...).

moosefs.com own repo

#### Operating system (distribution) and kernel version.

Fedora 44

## Describe the problem you observed.

I upgraded to Fedora 44 and had to uninstall moosefs-client because its dependency on `libfuse.so.3` could not be met:

```
Failed to resolve the transaction:
Problem: conflicting requests
  - nothing provides libfuse3.so.3()(64bit) needed by moosefs-client-4.58.4-1.rhsystemd.aarch64 from MooseFS
  - nothing provides libfuse3.so.3(FUSE_3.0)(64bit) needed by moosefs-client-4.58.4-1.rhsystemd.aarch64 from MooseFS
  - nothing provides libfuse3.so.3(FUSE_3.12)(64bit) needed by moosefs-client-4.58.4-1.rhsystemd.aarch64 from MooseFS
  - nothing provides libfuse3.so.3(FUSE_3.2)(64bit) needed by moosefs-client-4.58.4-1.rhsystemd.aarch64 from MooseFS
  - package moosefs-client-4.58.4-1.rhsystemd.x86_64 from MooseFS does not have a compatible architecture
  - nothing provides libfuse3.so.3()(64bit) needed by moosefs-client-4.58.4-1.rhsystemd.x86_64 from MooseFS
  - nothing provides libfuse3.so.3(FUSE_3.0)(64bit) needed by moosefs-client-4.58.4-1.rhsystemd.x86_64 from MooseFS
  - nothing provides libfuse3.so.3(FUSE_3.12)(64bit) needed by moosefs-client-4.58.4-1.rhsystemd.x86_64 from MooseFS
  - nothing provides libfuse3.so.3(FUSE_3.2)(64bit) needed by moosefs-client-4.58.4-1.rhsystemd.x86_64 from MooseFS
You can try to add to command line:
  --skip-broken to skip uninstallable packages
```

This is because Fedora 44 is now on `libfuse3.so.4`:
https://packages.fedoraproject.org/pkgs/fuse3/fuse3-libs/fedora-44.html#provides

title: [BUG] mfsmakesnapshot freezes filesystem until completed
author: lemker
body:
## Have you read through available documentation, open Github issues and Github Q&A Discussions?
Yes

## System information

#### Your moosefs version and its origin (moosefs.com, packaged by distro, built from source, ...).
4.58.2 PRO from moosefs.com

#### Operating system (distribution) and kernel version.
Ubuntu 24.04 LTS 6.8.0-63-generic

## Describe the problem you observed.

During `mfsmakesnapshot`, the entire filesystem is frozen until the snapshot is completed on "larger" directories. For instance, the following directory takes 6.35s to complete:

```
mfsdirinfo data/
data/:
 inodes:                    4383996
  directories:               225688
  files:                    4157894
 chunks:                   11106108
 length:            544354061525223
 size:              504541029564416
 realsize:          900001902145536
```

Is this filesystem freeze due to all inodes from MooseFS master memory being read and saved? Or can this time be improved? I am already snapshotting per subdirectory, not the entire filesystem.

title: [BUG] 4.58.1 PRO - mounting behaves weird
author: eleaner
body:
## Have you read through available documentation, open Github issues and Github Q&A Discussions?

Yes I believe

## System information

#### Your moosefs version and its origin (moosefs.com, packaged by distro, built from source, ...).
4.58.1 PRO
#### Operating system (distribution) and kernel version.
debian
#### Hardware / network configuration, and underlying filesystems on master, chunkservers, and clients.
various mostly older consumer hardware
#### How much data is tracked by moosefs master (order of magnitude)?

 - All fs objects: 3 135 348
 - Total space: 115TB
 - Free space: 23TB
 - RAM used: 1.8GB
 - last metadata save duration: 4.1

## Describe the problem you observed.
after upgrade to 4.58.1 PRO mfsmount stopped working (fstab)
my exports show
```
*                       /       rw,alldirs,admin,mingoal=2,maproot=0:0
*                       /photos_snap/photos rw,alldirs,admin,mingoal=2,mapall=1000:1000
*                       /others rw,alldirs,admin,mingoal=2,mapall=1000:1000
*                       /photos_snap/snaps ro,alldirs,admin,mingoal=2,mapall=1000:1000
```
my fstab
```
mfsmount /mnt/mfs        fuse defaults,mfsmaster=mfsmaster,mfsport=9421,mfsdelayedinit,mfspreflabels=N;S;R,allow_other,nosuid,nodev 0 0
mfsmount /mnt/mfs_photos fuse defaults,mfsmaster=mfsmaster,mfsport=9421,mfsdelayedinit,mfssubfolder=/photos_snap/photos,mfspreflabels=N;S;R,allow_other,nosuid,nodev 0 0
```
yet 
/mnt/mfs mounts /photos_snap/photos
and

tell me what to run

title: [BUG] Can set invalid label on storage class
author: edrock200
body:
## Have you read through available documentation, open Github issues and Github Q&A Discussions?

Yes

#### Your moosefs version and its origin (moosefs.com, packaged by distro, built from source, ...).
4.58
#### Operating system (distribution) and kernel version.
Ubuntu 24.04
#### Hardware / network configuration, and underlying filesystems on master, chunkservers, and clients.
Xfs
#### How much data is tracked by moosefs master (order of magnitude)?

 - All fs objects: 30m
 - Total space: 3pb
 - Free space: 1pb
 - RAM used: 26GB
 - last metadata save duration:
1h
## Describe the problem you observed.

#### Can you reproduce it? If so, describe how. If not, describe troubleshooting steps you took before opening the issue.
I went to change an archive label and archive mode from one storage class to another. I used the wrong type of single quotation marks when entering mfsscadmin modify -A '2A/[IP]' -o F class name except I used the other style of single quotes. 

Mfs accepted this, but when the confirmation spit out it showed the archive label as "-o". I immediately fixed this but when I went into mfs gui, it was orange showing that less than 1% of the data was in danger. It eventually counted down and went green again. I'm not sure what the ramifications would have been had it stayed in this non existent storage label. I've never seen the system warn of data at risk when changing storage class labels though, so I figured it was worth mentioning in case this is an actual bug.

title: [FEATURE] Documentation on what is 'grace mode' for chunkservers
author: JoaGamo
body:
## Have you read through available documentation, open Github issues and Github Ideas Discussions?

Yes

#### Your moosefs version and its origin (moosefs.com, packaged by distro, built from source, ...).

MooseFS 4.58.0

## Describe your request:

Hello!
'grace mode' is mentioned in the mfsmaster.cfg manpages [in this line](https://moosefs.com/manpages/mfsmaster-cfg#GENERAL%20NOTES)
> Whenever chunkserver load is higher than CS_HEAVY_LOAD_THRESHOLD and CS_HEAVY_LOAD_RATIO_THRESHOLD times higher than average load, then chunkserver is switched into 'grace' mode.

But it isn't mentioned in the rest of this entry (mfsmaster.cfg) or related entries of the man pages (like mfschunkserver, mfschunkserver.cfg) what is exactly 'grace mode'

I found this definition in the 1.7 (2013) changelog of MooseFS for the 'grace mode' of chunkservers. If it still is true, it should be mentioned

https://github.com/moosefs/moosefs/blob/583eb2688318a3f4c1625bb5f9ea7086201ec628/NEWS#L2514

title: [maybe BUG] 4.57.7: replication may not always be safe
author: onlyjob
body:
As per #709, I had millions of chunks moved to a dedicated chunkserver (on a stable reliable server with redundant storage). During replication, for unrelated reason I have normally rebooted machine with destination chunkserver. Once rebooted, one missing chunk appeared, from (unimportant) dataset that was in transition to rebooted machine. Chunkserver is configured with `HDD_FSYNC_BEFORE_CLOSE = 1` so no chunks should have been lost due to normal reboot.

Perhaps replication is not perfectly atomic/transactional, as apparently replicated chunk can be lost like that?

P.S. Replication reduced goal from two replicas to one.

title: [FEATURE] Fix TIME outdated docs on mfsexports.cfg default file
author: JoaGamo
body:
## Have you read through available documentation, open Github issues and Github Ideas Discussions?

Yes! I'm on MooseFS 4.57.7

Hello! The mfsexportfs.cfg file has outdated documentation.

the mfsexports.cfg file mentions this:
> mintrashretention=TIMEDURATION = do not allow to set trashretention below TIMEDURATION(TIMEDURATION **can be specified as number of seconds** or combination of elements #W,#D,#H in set order)
> maxtrashretention=TIMEDURATION = do not allow to set trashretention above TIMEDURATION (TIMEDURATION can be specified as above)

According to the manpages, TIME's minimum is 1 Hour, so no seconds or minutes.
> TIME can be specified as a number of hours (integer) or as a time period in one of two possible formats: first format: #.#T where T is one of: h-hours, d-days or w-weeks (...)

Before reading the manpage of mfstrashtime I tried adding as a parameter `maxtrashretention=10s` (which is a wrong TIME format but I don't get any logs for it) and I couldn't mount the exported directory on any client, for each client, `mfsmount` said "permission denied". That's why I think the comments on the mfsexports.cfg is wrong.

title: [BUG] 4.57.7: no replication to SC:1
author: onlyjob
body:
I re-defined storage class `1` as follows: `mfsscadmin modify -M /mnt/mfs -m strict -C W -K W  -T W 1`:

```
storage class changed: name: 1
        priority: 0 ; export_group: 1
        admin_only: NO ; labels_mode: STRICT ; arch_mode: C
        create_labels: W
        keep_labels: W
        arch_labels: not defined - using keep settings
        trash_labels: W ; min_trashretention: 0
```

in order to pin data to the only chunkserver labelled `W`. 
Then I changed storage class of some files/folders to `1` but nothing happened... "Redundancy" tab show number of files in that storage class, yet no chunks changed location and no data is flowing to `W` chunkserver.

title: [maybe BUG] one chink is always undergoal
author: onlyjob
body: For some reason, one chunk is always "undergoal" in my cluster (`4.57.7`), for months, and there is nothing special about the storage class (`-K U+P,M+P/[IP] -d 1h  -A U,M`). How to troubleshoot? Thanks.
